← Back to Main Report
Cosmetic / Workmanship Schedule — 152 Moverly Road, South Coogee
Defect Assessment  •  Cosmetic / Workmanship Schedule  •  v1.0

152 Moverly Road, South Coogee

Top (northern) townhouse — cosmetic, finishing & workmanship defects
ScopeCosmetic, finishing and workmanship items only
BasisVisual inspection from supplied photographs (~100 of 244 photos sampled)
Companion reportRead alongside Level 2: Upper Level / First Floor assessment (U1–U12)
SeverityMinor  •  Moderate  •  Verification required
⚠ Important: Findings are grouped by defect type so trades can be co-ordinated efficiently. Items tagged Verification required need a definitive site inspection before rectification. Where a cosmetic defect has a moderate functional consequence (weatherproofing, water ingress, structural movement), that concern takes precedence and the item should be resolved accordingly.
Note: Repeating items (paint scuffs, construction dust, tape residue) are listed indicatively. The same finding should be assumed to recur and a full handover-clean and touch-up programme applied, rather than treating the listed instances as the full count.

Contents

  1. APaint finish defects
  2. BExternal render defects
  3. CSealant and caulking defects
  4. DHandover cleaning items
  5. ETile, grout and stone defects
  6. FCarpet and soft-floor finishing
  7. GJoinery and cabinetry defects
  8. HGlass and glazing defects
  9. IHardware and metal finish defects
  10. JItems requiring investigation before rectification

APaint finish defects

Paintwork across the dwelling shows a consistent pattern of incomplete or low-quality finishing — runs, drips, missed touch-ups, exposed substrate at corners, ceiling overspray and roller-swirl marks. Best addressed by a single painter return after the handover clean.

A1

External door jamb — paint missing at head

Minor

Primary photo: P1140562

Location
External — Ground Floor entry door
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140562, P1140561

Observation

Paint film missing at the top of the jamb exposing bare or primed timber. Adjacent render and ceiling appear sound.

Recommendation

Sand, spot-prime and top-coat to match adjacent finish.

A2

External door jamb — paint flaking at sill

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140575

Location
External — door threshold (Ground Floor)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140575

Observation

Paint peeling off the jamb exposing bare timber at the sill. Consistent with moisture at the threshold — splash or capillary rise.

Recommendation

Strip loose paint; oil-based timber primer; top-coat. Investigate threshold detailing and rainwater management — paint failure of this kind indicates standing water at the sill.

A3

Door / window frame paint drips and ceiling overspray

Minor

Primary photo: P1140710

Location
Internal — stairwell / landing (to verify on site)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140710

Observation

Runs and drips visible on the frame; spatter dots on the adjacent ceiling plaster.

Recommendation

Sand runs on frame; spot-sand and touch-up ceiling spatters.

A4

Drywall corner bead not flushed; paint runs onto floor

Minor

Primary photo: P1140579

Location
Basement — lift lobby / circulation
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140579, P1140583, P1140584

Observation

Metal external corner bead is visible where setting compound has not fully covered it. Paint has run down the corner onto the concrete. Visible at more than one location.

Recommendation

Set and sand external angles to AS 2589.1 Level 4; re-prime, re-paint; clean paint run-off from concrete floor.

A5

Internal ceilings — roller swirl marks (not Level 4)

Minor

Primary photo: P1140693

Location
Upper Level — living area (and large open ceilings)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140692, P1140693

Observation

Visible roller swirls and drag marks; not a Level 4 paint finish. Most evident in raking light.

Recommendation

Light sand and re-coat ceilings, particularly in raking-light-prone rooms (living, dining, large open ceilings).

A6

Cabinetry / drawer paint chip at top edge

Minor

Primary photo: P1140703

Location
Upper Level — joinery (kitchen / WIR — to verify)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140703

Observation

Small chip or filled mark on the top edge of the painted joinery.

Recommendation

Fill, sand, brush- or spray-touch-up. Spray finish required if existing is 2-pac.

A7

Door jamb paint chip at base

Minor

Primary photo: P1140728

Location
Internal — door jamb (Ground Floor)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140728

Observation

Small paint chip at the base of the jamb exposing substrate.

Recommendation

Fill, sand, touch-up.

A8

External door hinge — paint runs down hinge and onto jamb

Minor

Primary photo: P1140747

Location
External — door hinge (Ground Floor)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140747

Observation

Paint drips running down the hardware and the painted jamb face at the hinge. Hinge had not been masked during painting.

Recommendation

Solvent-clean hinge; touch-up jamb paint.

A9

White 2-pac drawer face — orange-brown stain mark

Minor

Primary photo: P1140751

Location
Upper Level — kitchen / laundry joinery (to verify)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140751

Observation

Rust-coloured mark on a white painted drawer front.

Recommendation

Clean first — if mark does not lift, refinish or replace door (2-pac is not easily spot-touched).

A10

Wall base to tile — paint peeling at corner, exposing substrate

Minor

Primary photo: P1140767

Location
Ground Floor — bathroom / WC (tile pattern suggests wet area)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140767

Observation

Paint peeling back at the corner where a painted wall meets tiled floor; substrate visible.

Recommendation

Scrape loose paint; prime with wet-area-tolerant primer; top-coat.

A11

Walls dwelling-wide — light scuffs, handling marks and dust on paint

Minor (dwelling-wide)

Primary photo: P1140694

Location
Dwelling-wide — multiple rooms
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140689, P1140694, P1140697, P1140729, P1140737, P1140738

Observation

General wall scuffs, handling marks and fine dust on paint surfaces; smudges at switch and handle level. Typical pre-handover state.

Recommendation

Full handover wall clean by the builder; touch-up paint across all rooms in a single visit. Allow the builder's painter to revisit for patch-touch consistency.

A12

Parapet capping — paint scuffs and bumpy finish

Minor

Primary photo: P1140700

Location
External — Upper Level parapet
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140700

Observation

Paint finish on parapet capping is uneven and scuffed along the run.

Recommendation

Sand, spot-fill, re-paint.

A13

External column base — blue waterproofing visible under top-coat

Minor

Primary photo: P1140790

Location
External — column at planter / Ground Floor deck
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140790

Observation

Column has been top-coated white but a ring of blue waterproofing paint is still visible at the base. Painter's-tape flag still attached mid-column.

Recommendation

Remove tape flag. Feather-sand and top-coat the base ring so the waterproofing is not visible.

A14

Pool-side wall — blue/green paint splashes on top-coat

Minor

Primary photo: P1140697

Location
Pool deck — pool-side rendered wall
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140697

Observation

Teal-coloured splashes and spatter on the white wall, consistent with pool waterproofing paint not cleaned before the external top-coat.

Recommendation

Scrape or solvent-clean. If staining persists, blocking primer and repaint affected area.

A15

Pool-side wall — additional paint drip spots on top-coat

Minor

Primary photo: P1140768

Location
Pool deck — pool-side rendered wall (second area)
Group
Paint
Photo references
P1140768

Observation

Additional small paint spots on the top-coat reinforce the pattern seen at the pool-side wall.

Recommendation

Include the pool-deck-side wall in a single repaint scope (combine with A14 and J2).

BExternal render defects

Render junctions, edges and corners show a pattern of incomplete pointing, chips and rough finishes. Group all items for a single renderer return.

B1

External render edge at frame — ragged, foam visible

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140686

Location
External — deck-level sliding door reveal
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140685, P1140686

Observation

Render edge is feathered and chipped along the aluminium frame. Yellow expanding foam visible in the gap between render and frame with no neat pointing or sealant finish.

Recommendation

Trim foam back; point neatly; apply neutral-cure silicone sealant bead full perimeter and paint.

B2

External render at ground level — gouge / impact marks

Minor

Primary photo: P1140687

Location
External — wall at lawn / garden edge
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140687

Observation

Several horizontal scratches or gouges in the lower render. Consistent with garden-maintenance contact (mower or whipper-snipper).

Recommendation

Render patch, sand, repaint. Consider a mow-strip or paving detail in this zone.

B3

External soffit / wall junction — open crack along the line

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140578

Location
External — soffit-to-wall junction
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140577, P1140578

Observation

Linear crack along the wall/soffit junction with a small flake or bulge mid-span.

Recommendation

Rake out, apply flexible sealant, paint. Monitor on return visit; if the crack reopens, an expansion-capable sealant bead is warranted.

B4

Render corner chip at deck edge

Minor

Primary photo: P1140690

Location
External — deck-level corner
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140690

Observation

Small chip in render at external corner exposing underlying material.

Recommendation

Render patch, paint.

B5

External render — streak / stain on window reveal

Minor

Primary photo: P1140713

Location
External — window reveal; also Upper Level skylight perimeter (p1140732)
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140713, P1140732

Observation

Dark streak or stain on the rendered window reveal. Skylight-frame perimeter (p1140732) shows similar bleed-through staining at plaster edge — addressed alongside U4.

Recommendation

Clean; if mark does not lift, blocking primer and repaint. For skylight perimeter, stain-block, make-good to AS 2589.1 Level 4 and repaint.

B6

External soffit render — rough finish with drag marks

Minor

Primary photo: P1140734

Location
External — soffit / parapet underside
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140734

Observation

Render shows visible drag marks and uneven texture; not a consistent finish.

Recommendation

Sand and skim; re-paint. On-site decision whether a full skim coat is warranted given height and visibility.

B7

Soffit render — incomplete around downpipe / penetration

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140742

Location
External — soffit at downpipe penetration
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140742

Observation

Render around the penetration is missing or patchy; substrate visible through the gap.

Recommendation

Render make-good and paint; install neat collar or flashing at the penetration.

B8

External render — impact chips

Minor

Primary photo: P1140764

Location
External — wall (location to verify on site)
Group
Render
Photo references
P1140764

Observation

Two visible impact marks or chips in the render exposing substrate.

Recommendation

Render patch, spot-paint.

CSealant and caulking defects

Silicone and caulking work is inconsistent — missing beads, lumpy beads and unfilled corner joints. A caulking-trade return covering all items is the right approach.

C1

External door head — sealant missing

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140576

Location
External — door head (frame-to-render junction)
Group
Sealant
Photo references
P1140576

Observation

Open gap visible between render and aluminium frame at the head. No sealant bead.

Recommendation

Rake out any debris and apply neutral-cure silicone bead across the full frame head.

C2

Kitchen / wet-area benchtop — lumpy silicone bead

Minor

Primary photo: P1140740

Location
Upper Level — kitchen benchtop (stone-to-wall junction)
Group
Sealant
Photo references
P1140740

Observation

Yellow tape residue visible on the edge of the stone; silicone bead between stone and wall is lumpy and uneven.

Recommendation

Solvent-clean tape residue (test on offcut first — some silicone-neutralising solvents can damage natural stone sealers). Rake out the silicone bead and re-run with neutral-cure food-area-grade silicone tooled smooth.

C3

External door frame silicone bead — lumpy / uneven

Minor / Moderate

Primary photo: P1140765

Location
External — door frame to wall junction
Group
Sealant
Photo references
P1140765, P1140766

Observation

The silicone bead between frame and wall is messy with visible lumps and an uneven line.

Recommendation

Rake out existing bead, re-run with neutral-cure silicone tooled smooth.

C4

Tile-to-carpet transition — unfilled gap at corner

Minor

Primary photo: P1140770

Location
Internal — doorway threshold (tile-to-carpet)
Group
Sealant
Photo references
P1140770

Observation

Wall meets the floor transition with a visible unfilled gap at the corner. Carpet also shows yellow discoloration consistent with existing finding U7.

Recommendation

Caulk and make-good the corner. Carpet cleaning under U7 / F2.

C5

Skirting-to-timber-floor junction — unfilled gap at base

Minor

Primary photo: P1140753

Location
Upper Level — bedroom / carpeted room (to verify on site)
Group
Sealant
Photo references
P1140753

Observation

Small visible gap between skirting and timber floor; skirting not sitting flush.

Recommendation

Fix-down skirting; paintable caulk as finishing bead.

DHandover cleaning items

The dominant theme across the photos is that the handover clean has not yet been done. Items below should be addressed by a single builders'-clean contractor visit.

D1

Lift cabin ceiling — silicone / film residue around downlights

Minor

Primary photo: P1140585

Location
Lift cabin — ceiling (multiple stainless panels)
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140585, P1140586

Observation

White residue ring around ceiling-mounted downlights on the stainless panels. Consistent with silicone squeeze-out or protective-film adhesive not cleaned at commissioning. Visible on more than one panel.

Recommendation

Solvent-clean stainless steel panels using a non-acidic, non-chloride cleaner. Check all downlights in the cabin.

D2

Glass screen / balustrade — dust and fingerprint marks

Minor

Primary photo: P1140574

Location
Internal — glass screen / balustrade
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140574

Observation

Dust and smudging on the glass face, typical of pre-handover state.

Recommendation

Handover glass clean.

D3

Ground Floor tile — cleaning / sealer residue streaks

Minor

Primary photo: P1140569

Location
Ground Floor — entry / circulation tile floor
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140569

Observation

Light streaks consistent with sealer or cleaner residue not buffed off.

Recommendation

Polish or buff with appropriate pad and neutral cleaner. Test small area first for tile specification.

D4

Bronze window sills — paint / plaster splashes, dust in tracks

Minor

Primary photo: P1140780

Location
Ground Floor — bronze-framed windows (street and garden facing)
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140780, P1140761

Observation

Paint and setting-compound splashes visible on bronze sills; cobwebs and dust in frame tracks.

Recommendation

Handover clean of all bronze window sills and tracks. Vacuum frame tracks.

D5

Tile floor — paint / caulk drip spots

Minor

Primary photo: P1140745

Location
Ground Floor — tiled area (to verify exact room)
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140745

Observation

Small white drips and spots on the tile face.

Recommendation

Scrape or wash off with appropriate solvent. Care with grout — test first.

D6

Carpet — setting compound debris on trim and floor

Minor

Primary photo: P1140711

Location
Ground Floor — carpeted bedroom (to verify which)
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140711

Observation

Setting compound debris still on the trim above carpet edge.

Recommendation

Vacuum and wipe trim. Carpet re-tucking under F1.

D7

Pelmet / blind top track — masking tape residue

Minor

Primary photo: P1140739

Location
Upper Level — window with sheer curtain / blind track
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140739, P1140741

Observation

Yellow-green masking tape remnants on top of pelmet or blind track; dust and marks on top surface.

Recommendation

Peel tape residue, solvent-clean, handover-clean the top surfaces.

D8

Sliding door thresholds and tracks — construction dust and debris

Minor

Primary photo: P1140759

Location
Multiple — sliding door thresholds throughout dwelling
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140758, P1140759

Observation

Dust and fine debris in the sliding tracks and on stone thresholds; dirt visible around track seal.

Recommendation

Vacuum and wipe down all sliding tracks and thresholds before handover. Confirm track seals intact after cleaning.

D9

Roof / skylight area — gravel bag and debris left on flat roof

Minor / Moderate

Primary photo: P1140708

Location
Flat roof — adjacent to Upper Level skylight
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140707, P1140708

Observation

Partly-full gravel bag sitting on the roof surface immediately next to the skylight. Glass has surface debris.

Recommendation

Remove all construction debris from the roof. Clean skylight glazing. Confirm bag is not blocking drainage.

D10

Timber flooring — pencil marks / boot scuffs

Minor

Primary photo: P1140760

Location
Internal — oak timber floor (hallway / bedroom — to verify)
Group
Clean
Photo references
P1140760

Observation

Dark pencil or scuff marks on the oak floor surface.

Recommendation

Light buff with finish-friendly product. Local re-coat if marks are into the finish.

ETile, grout and stone defects

Internal grout, pool coping and stone elements all need targeted trade attention.

E1

Tile grout uneven / deep-set across joints

Minor

Primary photo: P1140717

Location
Ground Floor — tiled area (entry / bathroom)
Group
Tile
Photo references
P1140717

Observation

Grout lines are recessed unevenly with a gritty finish; bedding looks light.

Recommendation

Re-grout affected joints to a level, smooth finish per AS 3958.1, matching specified joint profile.

E2

Pool coping — efflorescence and lumpy grout between tiles

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140735

Location
Pool — coping tiles around pool edge
Group
Tile
Photo references
P1140735

Observation

White calcium efflorescence visible across multiple tiles; grout between tiles is lumpy and stepped. Dark stain on adjacent vertical wall.

Recommendation

Engage pool tiling contractor: clean efflorescence (weak acidic solution compatible with the tile); re-grout uneven joints; confirm waterproofing membrane behind coping tiles is intact. See Pool Disclaimer.

E3

Travertine element — mismatched void filler and edge chip

Minor

Primary photo: P1140750

Location
Internal — travertine feature wall (entry / hall — to verify)
Group
Stone
Photo references
P1140750, P1140749

Observation

Voids in travertine filled with filler not colour-matched to the stone; small edge chip visible.

Recommendation

Stone specialist to re-fill voids with colour-matched resin; dress edge chip.

FCarpet and soft-floor finishing

Edge tucking and cleanup at carpet transitions.

F1

Carpet poorly tucked at skirting; loose along edge

Minor

Primary photo: P1140711

Location
Ground Floor — carpeted bedroom (same room as D6)
Group
Carpet
Photo references
P1140711

Observation

Carpet is loose at the skirting with fibres pulled forward.

Recommendation

Re-tuck carpet edge per AS 2455.1. Combine with D6 trim clean.

F2

Tile-to-carpet transition — yellow discolouration / debris (links to U7)

Minor

Primary photo: P1140770

Location
Internal — doorway threshold (tile-to-carpet, same as C4)
Group
Carpet
Photo references
P1140770

Observation

Carpet shows yellow discolouration at the tile transition. Cross-references existing finding U7.

Recommendation

Carpet clean per existing finding U7. Caulk gap under C4.

GJoinery and cabinetry defects

Panel alignment and finish-out items in built-in joinery.

G1

Joinery veneer panels — gap between adjacent panels

Minor

Primary photo: P1140721

Location
Upper Level — built-in joinery (kitchen / WIR — to verify)
Group
Joinery
Photo references
P1140721

Observation

Visible gap between two adjacent timber veneer panels.

Recommendation

Re-fix panels to close the gap; if gap is fixed, infill with colour-matched timber filler.

G3

Ceiling downlight cut-out with no fitting installed

Minor

Primary photo: P1140720

Location
Upper Level — WIR / secondary room ceiling (to verify)
Group
Joinery
Photo references
P1140720

Observation

Ceiling cut-out is visible with no light fitting installed (insulation or ceiling space exposed).

Recommendation

Electrician to install the specified fitting, or cap the opening and make-good plaster to AS 2589.1 Level 4.

HGlass and glazing defects

Edge chip on a balustrade panel — needs glazier review before any repair.

H1

Glass balustrade panel — chip at top edge

Verification required

Primary photo: P1140727

Location
Internal — stair / void glass balustrade (linked to U8)
Group
Glass
Photo references
P1140727

Observation

Small edge chip on the top corner of a glass panel.

Recommendation

Edge chips on toughened glass can propagate. Engage glazier or installer; likely panel replacement if the chip is within the toughened-edge zone. Combine with U8 (panel-to-panel gap measurement).

IHardware and metal finish defects

Bronze frame finish touch-ups, repeating across multiple windows.

I1

Bronze aluminium window frame — paint chip / scratch on bronze finish

Minor

Primary photo: P1140712

Location
Ground Floor — multiple bronze-framed windows
Group
Hardware
Photo references
P1140712, P1140726

Observation

Visible marks or chips in bronze anodised finish on horizontal frame member. Appears at more than one window — repeating installation or handling issue.

Recommendation

Touch-up with matched bronze paint pen. If chip exposes underlying aluminium, treat with etch primer first.

JItems requiring investigation before rectification

These items are not 'fix and forget' — the visible defect is the symptom of something that needs to be confirmed first.

J1

Basement / garage polished concrete — linear crack through slab

Verification required

Primary photo: P1140571

Location
Basement — garage polished concrete floor
Group
Investigate
Photo references
P1140570, P1140571

Observation

Crack runs across the polished concrete floor visible as a linear discontinuity in the polished finish.

Recommendation

Engage a structural engineer to review. If confirmed inert (shrinkage), crack-inject with flexible epoxy and grind-polish to blend. If a movement crack, an induced control joint may be required as part of the repair.

J2

Pool-side wall — yellow / amber drip staining running down top-coat

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140689

Location
Pool deck — rendered wall adjacent to pool
Group
Investigate
Photo references
P1140689

Observation

Multiple yellow / amber streaks running vertically down the white painted wall. Pattern suggests wash from above (rain wash over a contaminated surface, or bleed-through from render compound).

Recommendation

Identify source (parapet capping wash, render-additive bleed-through, pool-chemical splash) before any repaint. If staining returns after cleaning, apply stain-blocking primer and repaint full wall panel. Combine with A14 and A15 in a single pool-side paint scope after diagnosis.

J3

High internal wall — dark drip / stain running down from cornice

Moderate

Primary photo: P1140755

Location
Internal — high wall in stairwell / void (to verify on site)
Group
Investigate
Photo references
P1140755

Observation

Dark drip mark runs vertically down the white internal wall from the cornice. Consistent with a water path — historic rain ingress past parapet capping or roof / gutter issue.

Recommendation

Inspect the parapet or roof edge directly above. If a water path is confirmed, this becomes a roof-plumbing item under the roof specialist disclaimer. Clean and repaint internal wall only after the source is rectified.

D5MinorCleanGround Floor — tiled area (to verify exact room)P1140745Tile floor — paint / caulk drip spots
D6MinorCleanGround Floor — carpeted bedroom (to verify which)P1140711Carpet — setting compound debris on trim and floor
D7MinorCleanUpper Level — window with sheer curtain / blind trackP1140739, P1140741Pelmet / blind top track — masking tape residue
D8MinorCleanMultiple — sliding door thresholds throughout dwellingP1140758, P1140759Sliding door thresholds and tracks — construction dust and debris
D9Minor / ModerateCleanFlat roof — adjacent to Upper Level skylightP1140707, P1140708Roof / skylight area — gravel bag and debris left on flat roof
D10MinorCleanInternal — oak timber floor (hallway / bedroom — to verify)P1140760Timber flooring — pencil marks / boot scuffs
E1MinorTileGround Floor — tiled area (entry / bathroom)P1140717Tile grout uneven / deep-set across joints
E2ModerateTilePool — coping tiles around pool edgeP1140735Pool coping — efflorescence and lumpy grout between tiles
E3MinorStoneInternal — travertine feature wall (entry / hall — to verify)P1140750, P1140749Travertine element — mismatched void filler and edge chip
F1MinorCarpetGround Floor — carpeted bedroom (same room as D6)P1140711Carpet poorly tucked at skirting; loose along edge
F2MinorCarpetInternal — doorway threshold (tile-to-carpet, same as C4)P1140770Tile-to-carpet transition — yellow discolouration / debris (links to U7)
G1MinorJoineryUpper Level — built-in joinery (kitchen / WIR — to verify)P1140721Joinery veneer panels — gap between adjacent panels
G3MinorJoineryUpper Level — WIR / secondary room ceiling (to verify)P1140720Ceiling downlight cut-out with no fitting installed
H1Verification requiredGlassInternal — stair / void glass balustrade (linked to U8)P1140727Glass balustrade panel — chip at top edge
I1MinorHardwareGround Floor — multiple bronze-framed windowsP1140712, P1140726Bronze aluminium window frame — paint chip / scratch on bronze finish
J1Verification requiredInvestigateBasement — garage polished concrete floorP1140570, P1140571Basement / garage polished concrete — linear crack through slab
J2ModerateInvestigatePool deck — rendered wall adjacent to poolP1140689Pool-side wall — yellow / amber drip staining running down top-coat
J3ModerateInvestigateInternal — high wall in stairwell / void (to verify on site)P1140755High internal wall — dark drip / stain running down from cornice

▶Recommended Rectification Approach

Items are best addressed as a co-ordinated handover programme. Proposed sequence below.

  1. Investigate first — Group J items (J1 slab crack, J2 pool-side staining source, J3 high-wall drip source). Repaint and tile work depending on these diagnoses should not start until they are confirmed.
  2. Builders' handover clean — Group D in a single visit. Walls, glass, tracks, sills, lift cabin, pelmets, tile floors, stone, thresholds, roof debris removal.
  3. Render / plaster return — Groups B and A4 (drywall corner beads). All render junctions, render chips, soffit cracks and corner-bead make-good in one visit.
  4. Caulking / sealant return — Group C. Re-run all silicone beads to external door frames, kitchen benchtop and frame junctions; close skirting-to-floor and tile-to-carpet gaps.
  5. Painter return — Group A in one visit after the clean. Wall touch-ups, ceiling re-coat where needed, parapet capping, hinge paint clean, column base finish, pool-side walls (after J2 diagnosis).
  6. Tiling return — Group E. Re-grout interior tiles; pool coping work in conjunction with pool waterproofing specialist.
  7. Joinery return — Group G items (panel gap; drawer-face refinishing where 2-pac touch-up is not workable).
  8. Glazier / installer review — Group H (balustrade edge chip) for advice on whether panel replacement is required. Tie in with U8.
  9. Hardware — Group I bronze-frame touch-ups can be done by the installer or as part of the painter return, depending on the matched paint product.

!Limitations

  • Schedule is based on a targeted sample of approximately 100 of the 244 inspection photos. Photos in the 587–684 range are largely covered in the Level 2 — Upper Level assessment (U1–U12) and any cosmetic items visible there are noted by cross-reference (U4, U6, U7, U8).
  • Severity ratings are based on photographic evidence alone. Items tagged Verification required (J1, J3, H1) need definitive site inspection before rectification.
  • Where a defect has a moderate functional consequence (weatherproofing, water ingress, structural movement), that concern takes precedence over the cosmetic classification.
Access Property Services  •  Cosmetic / Workmanship Schedule  •  152 Moverly Road
